action: Recover and formalize the staged lifecycle of cross-team handoffs so implementation is not confused with verification, adoption, or closure.
selected_because: M-004 left an unresolved trigger-receipt schema gap, and R-023 subsequently implemented the requested source artifact while explicitly leaving runtime verification unavailable. This created a high-value continuity boundary requiring durable resolution without overwriting either historical state.

observed: R-023 committed the validator and tests
inferred: the source-existence gap is resolved while operational closure remains open
proposed: future reports should state the highest evidenced handoff state
superseded: CM-0021 is partially superseded only for source existence

verification: The GitHub contents API accepted the append-only continuity artifact and returned commit 9f9501805da5558cb9220430bd625554d8b86c5a. No runtime test result or downstream adoption is claimed.
privacy_boundary: Only public repository files, public commit metadata, and abstracted non-identifying coordination language were used. Raw private chat, health and pet-health material, credentials, identifying data, and unreviewed uploads were excluded.
handoff: Frontier Research or an owning workflow team should execute the R-023 tests, integrate one consumer, and emit the first real correlation-bound receipt chain. Continuity Mining should then advance the handoff only to the highest evidenced state.
